rag_rules: Keep port-facing contract clauses, constants, and equations under a component type owner; keep implementation-only bindings under a component implementation owner.
A type owner's block may reference only symbols visible in that type; subcomponent ports or internal paths must stay in implementation scope or be exposed through an owned element first.
When attaching content to an implementation body, place it after the declarations it depends on, without disturbing existing subcomponents or connections.
generic_rules: Choose the owner by symbol visibility and scope, not by trying to consolidate all generated content into one place.
Preserve the existing AADL architecture during insertion: do not rename, remove, or alter features, subcomponents, connections, or property associations.
If type-scoped and implementation-scoped content both exist, keep them under separate matching owners rather than merging across scopes.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use the standard wrapper `annex agree {** ... **};` and keep the surrounding component or implementation syntax structurally balanced.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-only references in a type owner's block.
Do not create new architectural elements or move existing declarations just to accommodate generated contract text.
irrelevant_topics: Temporal recurrence or branching-expression syntax details.
